episode_summary: The team (with guest Ed Brooke-Hitching) discuss a veteran goat on Captain Cook’s voyage, Shakespeare’s King Lear spending 150 years with a happy ending, sneaky ad tricks like single-pixel “dust” to induce clicks, and a Victorian whistling oyster celebrity. Along the way they explore exploration history, censorship and rewrites, and quirky marketing stunts.

facts.fact: So my fact is the most experienced sailor of all Captain Cook's First Voyage was a goat.
My fact this week is that for 150 years, Shakespeare's play The Tragedy of King Lear had a happy ending.
My fact is that some advertisers have started putting single pixels on mobile phones ads so you think it's dust, try to wipe it off and accidentally click on the advert.
OK, my fact this week is that one of the most popular celebrities in London in 1860, was an oyster who could whistle.
